near_leaf_pairs.note: Same global-duration-positive house view on adjacent leaves. Both grounded in identical p.3 Central Narrative 'Yields' bullet ('markets likely to price too much tightening... bring yields down over time', PDF p.3 visual confirmed) reinforced by p.4 'the next rate move will be a cut'. Model rendered it as generic Duration O; GT rendered it as the specific DM-govt leaf US Treasuries O (also citing safe-haven status + flat 4.00% 10-yr forecast). Views agree; this is a leaf-granularity split of one call, not a conflict.

model_only_judgments.reason: Directly grounded in explicit prose, not a table delta: p.3 Central Narrative 'Yields — We believe markets are likely to price too much tightening based on rising oil prices, which should bring yields down over time' (verified against PDF p.3 visual, verbatim), corroborated by p.4 'the next rate move will be a cut'. A real global duration-positive house view that GT expressed only via its US Treasuries O row. Not review-flagged, acceptable given stated, unhedged prose grounding. Not one of the eliminated micro-delta rows.

gt_only_root_causes.note: Fully grounded in p.3 'yields down over time' + p.4 'next rate move will be a cut' + safe-haven flows; GT adds flat 4.00% 10-yr forecast (p.4/p.10). The model captured the identical call on the adjacent Duration leaf (model_only Duration O), so this is not a miss — a leaf-name divergence, the mirror image of pilot-05 where model Duration O was the orphan and GT US Treasuries O the covered side.

Grounded macro prose but no stated equity view: p.5 '4.5% to 5.0%' target, 'accumulated reserves should limit the near-term economic impact', 'weak domestic demand keeps the risk of deflation very much front of mind', China PPI chart deflationary. GT inferred an equity stance from country macro; the model emitted zero equity rows off the China section. Identical cause/leaf/view to pilot-05.

Grounded in p.2 Monetary Policy panel verbatim: 'The easing cycle that characterized 2025 seems mostly spent, but if energy prices stabilize there may be room for limited additional cuts in the US and UK that would bring policy back to a neutral setting.' GT inferred a cash-neutral stance; the 'cash yields attractive' phrasing is GT analyst gloss not in the document. Model emitted no cash row. Same cause/leaf/view as pilot-05.

special_audit.inferred_rows_verdict: All seven basis:inferred rows are sound single-step inferences from stated macro prose, every one agreeing with GT (RECALL-POSITIVE). Europe Duration N <- p.6 'ECB policy is at neutral and remains on hold for now'. Europe Equities U <- p.6 energy 'could annihilate' Germany's fiscal boost, 0.8% growth. UK Equities U <- p.7-8 unemployment 5.2%, '75,000 jobs lost', BoE extended hold, 0.7% growth. Japan Equities N <- p.8 'expansive fiscal policy will offset the direct impact of the energy shock' vs energy drag (balanced). Japan Govt Bonds U <- p.8 'BOJ will raise rates again' (policy 1.00->1.25). Emerging Markets Fixed Income U <- p.8-9 'easing largely priced out', stronger USD tightening, stagflation. EM Basket U <- p.9 'EM currency depreciation, capital outflows'. None are overreach.
special_audit.thin_rows_verdict: checker_strength:thin is the right grade for all five. USD O is 'stated' but genuinely hedged in source ('while the conflict remains intense... path thereafter is less clear', p.3), so capping at thin is correct. The four inferred U's (Europe Equities, UK Equities, EM FI, EM Basket) are single-step macro->positioning inferences with no explicit allocation verb, so thin/review is appropriately conservative. Well-calibrated.
special_audit.movement_vs_pilot05: Confirmed the intended headline movement. Pilot-05: AB emitted ~30 rows dominated by the p.10 forecast table converting 4-14bp deltas into views (the overreach tail), while the SAME country/macro equity calls were GT-only misses (inference_depth/not_emitted). Pilot-06: AB emitted only 10 rows, ZERO micro-delta table rows — the entire overreach tail is eliminated. Simultaneously the model MADE the previously-missed inferences: Japan Equities N, Europe Equities U, UK Equities U, Japan Govt Bonds U (prose-side, flipping from pilot-05's table N), EM Basket U (pilot-05 not_emitted) are now all exact matches. Net: recall lifted on 5+ former misses AND overreach source removed. Only China Equities N and Cash/Money Markets N remain residual inference_depth gaps.

notes: AB is the clean success story of this run and the clearest evidence the p.10-table overreach regime is fixed. It emitted 10 rows (9 exact_match, all view-agreeing; 1 model_only Duration O) versus ~30 last run, and NOT ONE is a forecast-table micro-delta — the pilot-05 overreach tail did not recur. The convention flipped the productive way: where pilot-05 followed the table and missed the prose, this run follows the prose and captures country macro->equity/rates inferences that were GT-only misses (Japan Eq, Europe Eq, UK Eq, Japan Govt Bonds, EM Basket now exact matches). All 7 inferred rows are sound single-step inferences agreeing with GT, and 'thin' is correctly applied (including hedged USD O). The single model_only, Duration O, is defensible_gt_omission — verbatim-grounded in the p.3 Central Narrative Yields bullet (confirmed against the PDF visual; snapshot de-scramble accurate) and covered by GT under US Treasuries O. The 3 gt_only rows are 1 near_leaf_covered (US Treasuries O) and 2 genuine inference_depth gaps (China Equities N, Cash/Money Markets N), both also missed in pilot-05 — the only persistent shortfall. No not_grounded or overreach model rows for AB this run.
